issues.claim: Analog not reconstructible from chapter alone
issues.objection: Removed-verse test for 7.22: if 7.22 were removed from the source pack, the chapter alone would not produce this specific engineering analog. The analog depends on the verse's specific structural claim (thread 3 (faith-made-firm continues; granting-agency is upstream)), which is not stated equivalently elsewhere in the chapter. Tags (operator-system-coupling, essence-vs-implementation, deliberate-action) anchor on this verse's specific claim. The STRETCHED tag is honest for this verse: Verse claims Krishna is the actual granter (mayaiva vihitān); engineering analog operates at proximate-vs-upstream attribution scope (the framework is the vehicle; the customer-need-and-substrate is what actually grants the result). Counter-example carves out accidental success without disciplined practice.

notes: Removed-verse test for 7.22: analog is anchored on this verse's specific structural claim, not on chapter-level material alone. PASS.
